summary: Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs across multiple Ukrainian regions, including Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Donetsk, and Dnipropetrovsk. Ukrainian forces reported numerous UAV incursions targeting settlements in Sumy, Poltava, Cherkasy, and Kharkiv oblasts. A missile strike in Kryvyi Rih caused three fatalities and 23 injuries.
key_points: Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aviation bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Donetsk, and Dnipropetrovsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/66086).
Ukrainian authorities recorded 1,050 attacks on 56 settlements in Zaporizhzhia region within one day, including 769 UAVs and 18 airstrikes (Source: @UkraineNow/82126).
Three civilians were killed and 23 wounded by a Russian missile attack in Kryvyi Rih (Source: @UkraineNow/82131).
UAV groups from the north of Sumy moved toward Chernihiv, targeting Ponornitsya and Holmy (Source: @kpszsu/66123).
Multiple UAV sightings reported in Poltava, Dnipropetrovsk, Cherkasy, and Sumy oblasts, often near specific villages (Source: @eRadarrua/97940, @eRadarrua/97933, @eRadarrua/97925).
Threats of Iskander-M and S-300 ballistic missile launches were announced from Voronezh, Kursk, and Crimea regions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68022, @mon1tor_ua/68029).
Russian forces engaged in combat near Zaporizhzhia's Lyubitske settlement (Source: @tass_agency/382113).

sections.executive_summary: Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs across Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Donetsk and Dnipropetrovsk, intensifying air‑ground pressure. Ukrainian forces reported extensive UAV activity targeting settlements in Sumy, Poltava, Cherkasy and Kharkiv oblasts. A missile strike in Kryvyi Rih resulted in three civilian deaths and 23 injuries.

points: Ukrainian authorities documented 1,050 attacks on 56 settlements in Zaporizhzhia region within a 24‑hour period, including 769 UAVs, 18 airstrikes, three multiple‑rocket launcher strikes and 260 artillery strikes, causing damage to housing and infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82126).
Three civilians were killed and 23 injured by a Russian missile strike in Kryvyi Rih; one of the wounded is in critical condition (Source: @UkraineNow/82131).
Ukrainian forces reported a group of UAVs from northern Sumy moving toward Chernihiv, targeting Ponornitsya and Holmy (Source: @kpszsu/66123).
In Poltava oblast, UAVs were observed near Dikan’ka and Gadyach, while in Dnipropetrovsk oblast they were seen near Samara, Podhorodne and near Shakhtarsk (Source: @eRadarrua/97898).
Ukrainian officials noted UAV activity in Cherkasy heading toward Korosten‑Shevchenkivskyi (Source: @eRadarrua/97933).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aviation bomb launches on Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Donetsk and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/66086).
Russian forces engaged in combat near the settlement of Lyubitske in Zaporizhzhia region (Source: @tass_agency/382113).
Threats of Iskander‑M and S‑300 ballistic missile launches were announced from Voronezh, Kursk and Crimea regions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68022, @mon1tor_ua/68029).
A Russian fuel truck reportedly evaded a Ukrainian drone (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31094).
Russian air assets were observed taking off MiG‑31K, indicating high‑altitude interception activity (Source: @kpszsu/66106).
UAVs from northern Sumy moved toward Chernihiv, targeting Ponornitsya and Holmy on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/66123).
Guided aviation bomb strikes were reported in Donetsk oblast, indicating Russian air activity on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/66086).
UAV activity reported near Pavlograd in eastern Dnipropetrovsk oblast (Source: @kpszsu/66109).
Russian tactical aviation also launched bomb strikes on Kharkiv oblast, intensifying pressure on the eastern sector (Source: @kpszsu/66098).
UAV sightings near Shakhtarsk and Samara in Dnipropetrovsk oblast suggest continued eastern reconnaissance missions (Source: @eRadarrua/97898).
UAVs were observed traveling from northern Sumy toward Chernihiv, indicating activity on the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/66123).
A UAV was reported near the village of Bliznyuky in Kharkiv oblast, close to the northern border (Source: @kpszsu/66093).
UAVs detected near the city of Shostka in Sumy oblast, suggesting northern sector incursions (Source: @eRadarrua/97916).
Russian tactical aviation bombings were reported in Kharkiv oblast, affecting the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/66098).
MiG‑31K take‑off was noted, reflecting Russian air operations that could impact the northern theater (Source: @kpszsu/66106).
Russian tactical aviation bomb strikes hit Zaporizhzhia oblast, including the city of Kryvyi Rih where a missile attack caused civilian casualties (Source: @kpszsu/66086, @UkraineNow/82131).
UAVs were reported near the Black Sea, heading toward Mykolaiv oblast from the sea (Source: @kpszsu/66095).
Threats of Iskander‑M missile launches were announced from Crimea, posing a risk to southern Ukrainian territories (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68022).
UAV activity observed near the coastal area of the Black Sea opposite Chornomorske (Source: @eRadarrua/97925).
Russian forces engaged near Lyubitske in Zaporizhzhia, a southern frontline settlement (Source: @tass_agency/382113).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs across multiple oblasts, including Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Donetsk and Dnipropetrovsk (Source: @kpszsu/66086).
Ukrainian forces reported 769 UAVs of various types, predominantly FPV, operating over Zaporizhzhia region in a single day (Source: @UkraineNow/82126).
MiG‑31K take‑off was recorded, indicating high‑altitude Russian interceptor activity (Source: @kpszsu/66106).
UAV sightings were widespread across Poltava, Cherkasy, Sumy, and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, targeting both civilian and military sites (Source: @eRadarrua/97940, @eRadarrua/97933).

Russian threats of Iskander‑M and S‑300 ballistic missile launches from Voronezh, Kursk and Crimea suggest potential escalation of the air‑to‑ground threat (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68022, @mon1tor_ua/68029).

key_developments: Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Donetsk and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, intensifying air‑ground operations (Source: @kpszsu/66086).
Ukrainian authorities recorded 1,050 attacks on 56 settlements in Zaporizhzhia region, including 769 UAVs and 18 airstrikes in 24 hours (Source: @UkraineNow/82126).
A missile strike in Kryvyi Rih resulted in three civilian deaths and 23 injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82131).
UAV groups from northern Sumy moved toward Chernihiv, targeting Ponornitsya and Holmy (Source: @kpszsu/66123).
Threats of Iskander‑M and S‑300 ballistic missile launches were announced from Voronezh, Kursk and Crimea (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68022, @mon1tor_ua/68029).
Russian forces engaged in combat near Lyubitske settlement in Zaporizhzhia region (Source: @tass_agency/382113).
MiG‑31K take‑off observed, indicating high‑altitude Russian interceptor activity (Source: @kpszsu/66106).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian air operations, including guided bomb deployments and potential ballistic missile launches, are elevating the threat to Ukrainian rear areas across multiple fronts. UAV incursions remain pervasive, targeting both civilian infrastructure and military positions in Sumy, Poltava, Cherkasy and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, complicating air defense allocations. The missile strike in Kryvyi Rih underscores the continued risk to densely populated centers, while threats from Iskander‑M and S‑300 systems could introduce longer‑range strike capabilities against strategic assets.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian regional commands have reported heightened alert levels, mobilizing air defense units to intercept UAV swarms and preparing for possible ballistic missile alerts. Local authorities in Zaporizhzhia have documented extensive damage and are coordinating emergency response for civilian casualties. In the north, Ukrainian forces are tracking UAV trajectories from Sumy toward Chernihiv to pre‑empt further incursions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source monitoring indicates a pattern of Russian tactical aviation strikes synchronized with UAV saturation attacks, suggesting a combined‑arms approach to overwhelm Ukrainian defenses. The announced threats of Iskander‑M launches from multiple Russian regions may be a strategic signaling effort rather than immediate deployment, but require continuous radar vigilance. Continued observation of UAV launch points in Russian‑occupied territories will be critical for forecasting future attack vectors.
